subject: English
skill: Narrative Writing With Dialogue and Description

lesson.story_mission: Join the Story Trail crew to shape events, dialogue, and description into Grade 4 narratives.
lesson.objective: I can sequence events, add description, use dialogue and transitions, and write a clear narrative closure.
lesson.concept_explanation: Narrative writing tells events in order, uses description and dialogue to show characters, includes transitions, and ends with closure.
lesson.common_misconception: Some writers list events without order, description, dialogue punctuation, or a satisfying ending.
lesson.real_world_connection: Grade 4 writers use this skill for reading responses, science notes, social studies projects, and stories classmates can follow.
lesson.lesson_summary: Narrative writing tells events in order, uses description and dialogue to show characters, includes transitions, and ends with closure.
lesson.mini_lesson: Read the prompt, plan the needed parts, draft a clear answer, and reread with the checklist before you submit.

story.text: Read This Page. Welcome to the Grade 4 writing studio for Narrative Writing With Dialogue and Description. You will plan, test, revise, and explain choices the way a real author does. Press Start Mission when you are ready to build a clear response.

lesson.text: Read This Page. This mini lesson teaches i can sequence events, add description, use dialogue and transitions, and write a clear narrative closure. First, study the model. Then name the parts that make the writing or sentence work. Press Next after you can explain the strategy in your own words.

watch.text: Read This Page. On this Watch page, listen to the teacher think aloud. Notice how the model checks the prompt, chooses useful words, and avoids a common mistake. Press Next to try a supported demo.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: Read This Page. This demo is a safe practice round. Read the sentence or paragraph aloud, point to the evidence in the visual model, and decide what needs to be added or revised. Press Next when you are ready for Practice.

practice.text: Read This Page. Practice time. Read the item, use the visual model, and press Read Question if you want the exact prompt read aloud. Use Listen for model sentences, paragraph examples, dialogue, or grammar support before you answer.

challenge.text: Read This Page. Challenge time asks you to use i can sequence events, add description, use dialogue and transitions, and write a clear narrative closure with less help.
